title: Senior / Patient Service Associate (Diagnostic Radiology)
description:
Woodlands Health Campus (WHC) envisions personalised and quality healthcare for the community of the future. It will co-develop and integrate services with community stakeholders, and extend care beyond the hospital into the community as well as homes.

To maintain a high level of service quality at the reception and appointment hotline /call centre and to assist the DDR Manager and the Nurse Clinician in the daily running of DDR. To maintain a high level of service quality to patients and to ensure continuous improvement to work processes.

  • To receive and greet patients at various reception areas in DDR
  • To register patient manually or electronically on SAP and RIS
  • To print out patients’ labels, queue and time chits for appointments.
  • To handle all image duplication in DVDs/CDs and/or Radiological Reports in accordance to PDPA Act
  • To schedule appointments for outpatients and inpatients in accordance to scheduling rules set by DDR
  • To receive and sort out request forms from Wards/SOC for vetting by Radiographers and/or Radiologists
  • To offer simple financial advice on estimated procedure costs and service redirection of patients for financial assistance and counselling
  • To maintain and manage the reception areas in terms of stocking of stationery, relevant patient information sheets, presentation and cleanliness
  • To troubleshoot and manage cash machine for automatic payments
  • To handle manual cash payments and other relevant billing issues such as Medisave, drop charges, modification of charges, refunds or exam cancellations
  • To perform simple crowd-control and manage waiting times
  • To reduce no-show rates and communicate need for rescheduling to patients or next-of-kin
  • To manage patients during down-time as per Supervisor’s instructions e.g. rescheduling of appointments
  • To attend to telephone calls, fax and other enquiries from various sources
  • To participate in corporate events when necessary, especially those relevant in service quality
  • To undertake any other tasks as assigned by the Supervisor, Radiographers and Nurses

Requirements

  • Minimum Diploma OR GCE 'N/O/A/NITEC/ITE' Levels or WSQ Certificate in Healthcare (Admin Support)
  • Prior Experience in patient service in Hospital settings is an advantage
  • Good communication and interpersonal skills
  • Experience in healthcare settings will be advantage
  • Skilled in relevant Radiology IT systems such as RIS/PACS, SAP, EPIC will be an advantage
  • Skilled in Microsoft Office programmes such as Word and Excel

Please be informed that only application submitted through NHG Career Webpage (corp.nhg.com.sg/Careers), WHC Career Webpage (whc.sg/joinus), Jobstreet Singapore (jobstreet.com.sg), Singapore Medical Association (sma.org.sg), MyCareersFuture.sg, LinkedIn and WHC appointed recruitment agencies will be considered. If you are accessing this job posting from another recruitment channel, please visit the official channels mentioned above.

postedCompany.description:
Woodlands Health Campus (WHC) broke ground on 18 April 2017. The 1,800-bedded facility will comprise a fully-integrated Acute and Community Hospital, Specialist Outpatient Clinics and an Intermediate & Long-Term Care facility. Our vision for the single-build Campus will have both acute and community hospitals sharing the same building and medical teams. This will allow patients to move seamlessly from acute to rehabilitative care, with a unified care team.

Our 'future-ready' Campus will employ practical technology to augment manpower needs to deliver care that is safe and more effective. Data analytics, telehealth, automation of manual processes or logistics robots are just some of the potential solutions WHC will use.

We are already serving patients in our pre-operations wards located at the Yishun Health campus. Be part of a team providing person-centric, quality care to improve the lives of our patients, community and healthcare family.

Similarly, our work in the community has already begun. Since 2015, we have been working with community care providers to create an ecosystem of support for some 600,000 residents in the North of Singapore. We work closely with stakeholders including schools, Voluntary Welfare Organisations, and social and primary care providers to develop initiatives on health promotion, education and engagement.
postedCompany.name: WOODLANDSHEALTH PTE. LTD.
